questions.question: What is the WLC service port used for?
options: Out-of-band management; supports one VLAN only; connects to a switch access port
The primary data-plane traffic between APs and WLC
questions.explanation: The service port is a dedicated management port for out-of-band management; supports one VLAN only; connects to a switch access port.

questions.question: Which WLC port is the standard data-plane network port?
questions.explanation: The distribution system port is the standard data-plane network port on the WLC; connects to a switch trunk port; multiple can be combined into a LAG.

questions.question: What LAG mode do WLCs support?
options: Static LAG only (mode on; no LACP/PAgP)
questions.explanation: WLCs support static LAG only (mode on; no LACP/PAgP).

questions.question: What does PoE stand for?
questions.explanation: PoE stands for Power over Ethernet — delivers power to APs (or WLCs) over the same Ethernet cable that carries data.

questions.question: How does a lightweight AP connect to the switch?
options: Via an access port; all traffic is tunneled to the WLC via CAPWAP
Via a trunk port carrying one VLAN per SSID
questions.explanation: Lightweight AP connection uses a switch access port; all traffic is tunneled to the WLC via CAPWAP.

questions.question: How does an autonomous AP connect to the switch?
options: Trunk link carrying one VLAN per SSID plus management VLAN
Access port with traffic tunneled elsewhere
Direct fiber link to the WLC
questions.explanation: Autonomous AP connection is a trunk link to switch carrying one VLAN per SSID plus management VLAN.

questions.question: What must a trunk to a WLC allow?
options: The management VLAN plus every VLAN mapped to a WLAN on the WLC
questions.explanation: The trunk to WLC must allow the management VLAN plus every VLAN mapped to a WLAN on the WLC.

questions.question: What does DHCP option 43 provide?
options: Tells lightweight APs the WLC's IP address (when WLC is in a different subnet)
Assigns a default gateway to APs
Provides the DNS server to WLCs
Configures the NTP server for APs
questions.explanation: DHCP option 43 tells lightweight APs the WLC's IP address (relevant when the WLC is in a different subnet).

questions.question: What is a 'stretched VLAN' and why is it bad practice?
options: Same VLAN spanning the whole LAN; causes large broadcast domains and STP-blocked links
A VLAN that spans multiple buildings via a routed core
A VLAN with more than 1000 hosts
A VLAN that crosses an ISP boundary
questions.explanation: Stretched VLAN (bad practice) is the same VLAN spanning the whole LAN (required with autonomous APs); causes large broadcast domains and STP-blocked links.

questions.question: Which switch command is required on the WLC-facing LAG interface?
questions.explanation: WLCs support only static LAG, so the switch side must use `channel-group 1 mode on`.

questions.question: What is a management VLAN?
options: Dedicated VLAN used only for device management traffic, kept separate from data
The default VLAN 1 used for all control plane traffic
A VLAN reserved for QoS tagging
A VLAN that carries only CAPWAP tunnels
questions.explanation: Management VLAN is a dedicated VLAN used only for device management traffic (WLC, APs, switches); kept separate from data traffic.

questions.question: What is the redundancy port on a WLC used for?
options: To connect two WLCs to form an HA pair
questions.explanation: The redundancy port is used to connect two WLCs to form an HA pair.
